events.text: Patient is a 55-year-old female of White/Caucasian ethnicity, Ashkenazi Jewish background, BMI 25.8, with a family history of colon cancer (mother diagnosed at age 60) and a personal history of anxiety disorder.
At age 54, patient underwent screening colonoscopy which revealed a 4 cm mass in the sigmoid colon (C18.7); she had been asymptomatic.
Biopsy of sigmoid colon mass showed moderately to poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma (histology 8140/3) with lymphovascular invasion identified.

Comprehensive genomic profiling demonstrated KRAS G12D mutation, BRAF wild-type, PIK3CA wild-type, APC R1450* nonsense mutation, TP53 Y220C mutation; microsatellite stable (MSS); tumor mutational burden 6 mutations/Mb; no NTRK fusions detected.

Staging PET/CT at age 54 showed FDG-avid sigmoid colon mass (SUVmax 14.2), two FDG-avid hepatic lesions in segment IVb (2.3 cm, SUVmax 8.7) and segment VII (1.8 cm, SUVmax 7.1), and FDG-avid mesenteric lymph nodes; no distant disease beyond liver; staged T3N2aM1a.

At age 54, baseline CEA was 89 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 54 U/mL; oligometastatic liver disease with left-sided primary; tumor board recommended systemic therapy with reassessment for liver-directed surgery.
At age 54, initiated first-line FOLFOX plus bevacizumab every 2 weeks.

After cycle 1 of FOLFOX, patient experienced grade 2 nausea and cold-induced dysesthesia in hands characteristic of acute oxaliplatin toxicity; managed with dose-adjusted anti-emetics and cold avoidance counseling.

Restaging CT after 6 cycles of FOLFOX-bevacizumab demonstrated partial response with hepatic lesion in segment IVb decreased to 1.4 cm and segment VII lesion decreased to 0.9 cm; sigmoid primary also decreased in size; CEA 22 ng/mL.
At age 55, tumor board deemed patient a candidate for hepatic metastasectomy given good response and limited liver disease burden.
At age 55, patient underwent laparoscopic sigmoid colectomy with high ligation of inferior mesenteric artery.

Sigmoid colectomy specimen showed residual moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma with treatment effect (30% viable tumor), ypT3N1a (1 of 19 lymph nodes positive), negative proximal and distal margins.
At age 55, staged hepatic metastasectomy performed 6 weeks after colectomy; segment IVb segmentectomy and segment VII wedge resection completed with intraoperative ultrasound guidance.
Hepatic resection specimens showed treated adenocarcinoma metastases with 50% viable tumor in segment IVb lesion and 20% viable tumor in segment VII lesion; all margins negative (R0).
At age 55, postoperative FOLFOX resumed for 4 cycles to complete perioperative chemotherapy plan; bevacizumab held for 6 weeks postoperatively then restarted.
At age 55, patient developed grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy after 10 total cycles of oxaliplatin; oxaliplatin discontinued for final 2 planned cycles, completed with 5-FU/leucovorin alone.
Surveillance CT at age 55 showed post-surgical changes without evidence of recurrent disease; CEA was 4.8 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 19 U/mL.
At age 55, patient experienced significant anxiety regarding cancer recurrence requiring adjustment of psychiatric medications and referral to psycho-oncology.

At age 55, entered surveillance with CEA every 3 months, CT every 3-6 months, and colonoscopy at 1 year; genetic counseling completed and germline testing negative for Lynch syndrome and other hereditary cancer syndromes.
CT at age 55 (6 months post-surgery) showed no evidence of recurrent disease; CEA was 3.2 ng/mL and CA 19-9 was 14 U/mL; neuropathy improving to grade 1.

scenario_blurb: Stage IV colorectal cancer with KRAS G12D mutation. Left-sided and right-sided primary tumors. Treated with FOLFOX or FOLFIRI plus bevacizumab. Some with liver-limited metastases considered for hepatic resection. MSI/MMR testing performed.
